One-Line Description: User/session auth and access control across the app
Key Gaps: No login, no tenancy, no authorization, and no per-user data isolation

One-Line Description: Planned stock screener page and backend query engine
Key Gaps: Pure placeholder page with no backend or data pipeline

One-Line Description: Separate AI chat page outside the advisor workflow
Key Gaps: Returns scaffold text only; no real model execution or portfolio context

One-Line Description: Connect broker accounts and sync holdings into a portfolio
Key Gaps: Feature flag disabled and connectors are scaffolded only

One-Line Description: Standalone frontier page and deprecated frontier endpoint
Key Gaps: Real optimizer exists elsewhere but this surface is still coming-soon/scaffolded

One-Line Description: Legacy mock mode removal from real runtime flows
Key Gaps: Runtime avoids mock mode but stale schemas, types, docs, and UI references remain

One-Line Description: Corporate events timeline alongside portfolio news
Key Gaps: Events are usually empty because no real corporate calendar integration exists

One-Line Description: Portfolio-relevant news feed for holdings and market context
Key Gaps: Usually empty without NEWS_API_KEY and backend still carries scaffold-era behavior

One-Line Description: Automated confidence layer for backend and frontend workflows
Key Gaps: Backend contract tests exist, but there are no meaningful frontend tests or end-to-end browser tests

One-Line Description: Client-side portfolio sandbox for weight changes and what-if scenarios
Key Gaps: Mostly frontend sandbox, not persisted, and not backed by a dedicated simulation service

One-Line Description: Portfolio Q&A using AI when available or rules otherwise
Key Gaps: True AI depends on provider keys and package availability; otherwise it degrades to rule-based responses

One-Line Description: Compare a holding against industry peers across fundamentals
Key Gaps: Fundamentals are real but peer universe discovery is mostly a static curated map

One-Line Description: Async enrichment of names, sectors, and fundamentals after import
Key Gaps: Runs in background but the upload UI does not actively poll and surface completion/failure states

One-Line Description: Primary market entry page with indices, movers, and headlines
Key Gaps: Indices and movers are real, but headlines and some asset blocks are placeholders

One-Line Description: Fetch and apply live prices to uploaded holdings
Key Gaps: Coverage depends on symbol quality and can fall back to average cost, which hides stale pricing

One-Line Description: Per-holding and weighted valuation, quality, growth, and leverage metrics
Key Gaps: Coverage gaps remain for unsupported tickers and unavailable Yahoo/FMP results

Feature / Workflow: Error handling / loading states / empty states
One-Line Description: Graceful UI behavior during load, failure, stale, and empty conditions
Key Gaps: Some failures are softened into empty or stale states, which can hide underlying data/provider problems

One-Line Description: Compute frontier, min-variance, max-Sharpe, and rebalance suggestions
Key Gaps: Quality depends on price history coverage and may use Monte Carlo fallback when scipy is unavailable

One-Line Description: Replace holdings in an existing portfolio with a fresh file import
Key Gaps: Workflow works, but background progress and post-refresh trust indicators are limited

One-Line Description: Compute portfolio risk, benchmark comparison, drawdown, and correlation
Key Gaps: Advanced metrics require usable price history and degrade sharply for bad or unsupported symbols

One-Line Description: Central frontend transport and typed API wrapper layer
Key Gaps: Well centralized, but still exposes deprecated and legacy routes and stale mock/frontier type hints

One-Line Description: Capture portfolio snapshots, build daily history, and compare changes over time
Key Gaps: Core flow works, but snapshot risk payloads are not actually persisted and history build is async/failure-prone

One-Line Description: Store and display sector allocations and sector-level portfolio drift
Key Gaps: Unknown sectors remain when enrichment cannot resolve ticker metadata

One-Line Description: Show holding-level P&L against average purchase cost
Key Gaps: Accuracy depends on current_price freshness because current values are reused rather than independently refreshed

One-Line Description: List, activate, rename, delete, and inspect portfolios
Key Gaps: Works in a single global app context, not in a user-scoped multi-portfolio environment

One-Line Description: Main summary dashboard driven by the active stored portfolio
Key Gaps: Commentary is supplemental and can silently drop out without surfacing an explicit status

One-Line Description: Feature flags and provider keys define runtime capabilities
Key Gaps: Good centralization, but optional-provider absence is often only discovered at runtime

One-Line Description: CRUD watchlist with cached state and optional live quotes
Key Gaps: No user isolation; live quotes are non-critical and can fail silently

One-Line Description: Display holdings with weights, P&L, and portfolio-level filtering
Key Gaps: Output quality still depends on enrichment and pricing quality for each ticker

One-Line Description: Detect canonical columns and let users fix them before import
Key Gaps: Heuristic detection still relies on user correction for irregular broker exports

One-Line Description: Upload wizard for CSV/XLSX portfolio files
Key Gaps: Import succeeds quickly, but async enrichment visibility is weaker than the UX implies

One-Line Description: Normalize valid rows and persist a canonical active uploaded portfolio
Key Gaps: Strong core flow, but still single-user and dependent on downstream enrichment for full fidelity
